claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 17 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 17 and in the 20-week extension period
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 17 and in the 20-week extension period; Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is 17 and in the 20-week extension period
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit.

To get started, could you please tell me:

**How many children are you inquiring about, and what are their names?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! I'm inquiring about my child, Alex. Just the one.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! So I'm assessing eligibility for your child, Alex.

Before I can determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit, I need to gather some details about Alex's circumstances.

**How old is Alex, or what is Alex's date of birth?**

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. Now I need to understand a bit more about Alex's current situation.

**Does Alex live with you?**

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Good, thank you. I need to check a few more important details.

**Is Alex in full-time education or approved training?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Well, Alex actually left school recently, but they've just signed up with the careers service to figure out what to do next.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for clarifying that. I need to gather the information from the official government guidance to make sure I assess this correctly.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Based on the guidance, I now need to ask one more important question to complete my assessment:

**Are you a British citizen or do you have the right to reside in the UK (for example, through settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme, or as a Crown servant)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in Manchester and I'm a British citizen.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for confirming that. I now have all the information I need to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

Based on the government's eligibility rules, I can provide you with my assessment:

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Great news! You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

Here's why:

- **Alex's age and registration:** Although Alex is 17 and has left school, the rules specifically allow for a 20-week extension of Child Benefit payments when a 16 or 17 year old registers with a government-sponsored careers service after leaving education. Since Alex has just signed up with the careers service, this extension applies.

- **Living arrangements:** Alex lives with you, so you meet the responsibility requirement.

- **Your circumstances:** As a British citizen living in the UK, you meet the residency requirements.

You should be able to claim Child Benefit for Alex for the next 20 weeks. If Alex enters approved education or training during this period, your eligibility could potentially continue beyond the 20 weeks. If you have any further questions or your circumstances change, you can contact the Child Benefit Office for more guidance.

reasonings: Alex is 17 years old and lives with you in the UK. According to the eligibility rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16. However, the rules state that you'll normally still qualify for Child Benefit if your child is under 20 and they stay in approved education or training. Additionally, there is a provision that allows you to apply for Child Benefit to continue for 20 weeks if a 16 or 17 year old leaves education or training and registers with either a government-sponsored careers service or the armed services. Since Alex has just left school and registered with the careers service (a government-sponsored careers service), Alex qualifies for the 20-week extension period. You are a British citizen living in the UK, meeting the residency requirements. As you live with Alex and are responsible for them, you meet the responsibility criteria. Therefore, you are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex. Alex is 17 years old, lives with you, and has recently registered with a government-sponsored careers service after leaving school. This means you qualify for a 20-week extension of Child Benefit payments. As a British citizen living in the UK with responsibility for Alex, all eligibility criteria are met.

Since Alex has just left school and registered with the careers service (a government-sponsored careers service), Alex qualifies for the 20-week extension period. Therefore, you are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

This means you qualify for a 20-week extension of Child Benefit payments. As a British citizen living in the UK with responsibility for Alex, all eligibility criteria are met.
